video:video.video:title: US launches latest airstrikes against Iran while another tanker hit in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The United States conducted yet another round of airstrikes targeting Iran on Tuesday. The strikes came as Iran attacked a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz early Tuesday, forcing its crew to abandon the ship

video:video.video:title: The refuge for butterflies struggling to survive extreme heat
video:video.video:description: Butterflies are at threat from climate change as extreme temperatures disrupt every stage of their life cycle.

video:video.video:title: Yemen's Iranian-backed Houthi rebels say they will block Saudi shipping in Red Sea
video:video.video:description: Bab el-Mandeb, at the southern tip of the Arabian Peninsula, is the gateway to the Red Sea through which around 12% of the world's trade usually passes.

video:video.video:title: A Minnesota town now boasts the world's tallest nutcracker
video:video.video:description: The small town of Luverne now hosts a 65-foot tall nutcracker, decked out like Uncle Sam in honor of the nation’s 250th anniversary. (AP Video by Sarah Raza)

video:video.video:title: India police use tear gas to stop march on Parliament by Cockroach movement supporters
video:video.video:description: Police used tear gas and batons to stop supporters of India’s youth-led Cockroach movement from marching to Parliament on Monday to demand the education minister’s resignation, while the government appeared to make its first outreach to the movement’s leaders.

video:video.video:title: The Tate brothers fight extradition to the UK where they face rape and sex trafficking charges
video:video.video:description: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate will fight extradition to the United Kingdom, where the brothers are accused of rape and sex trafficking.

video:video.video:title: US actions harm global efforts to prevent and treat HIV in dozens of countries, new report says
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration's cuts to the U.S. Agency for International Development and thousands of global health grants have forced organizations to close clinics and suspend services to communities in need.

video:video.video:title: Ski enthusiasts flock to sub-Saharan Africa and a snowy mountain resort in Lesotho
video:video.video:description: One may not expect thousands to flock each winter to Lesotho but for many ski enthusiasts from across southern Africa, the small, landlocked mountain kingdom has become the go-to place in recent years.

video:video.video:title: 'Package deal': Zendaya says 'Spider-Man' cast can't be separated
video:video.video:description: 'Package deal': Zendaya says 'Spider-Man' cast can't be separated

video:video.video:title: Relatives mourn parents and children killed in Israeli airstrike on Gaza City
video:video.video:description: Relatives mourned a Palestinian couple and their four children who were killed by an Israeli airstrike in Gaza City on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Philippines accuses Chinese coast guard of injuring Filipino sailor in disputed shoal
video:video.video:description: The Philippine military on Monday accused China's coast guard of allegedly injuring a Filipino sailor during an incident in the disputed Second Thomas Shoal, locally known as Ayungin Shoal, in the South China Sea, saying that the Chinese coast guard personnel aboard a motorboat repeatedly struck the

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of deadly daytime Russian guided aerial bomb attack on Zaporizhzhia
video:video.video:description: At least three people were killed and another 13 injured following a daytime Russian guided aerial bomb attack on Zaporizhzhia on Tuesday, local authorities reported.

video:video.video:title: Lebanon's president meets with Trump at White House
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump pledged to help Lebanon during a White House meeting on Tuesday with the country's leader, Joseph Aoun, who came to push for long-term calm after months of war between Israel and the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group.

video:video.video:title: Trump’s new Canada tariffs could bring uncertainty, automotive industry group official says
video:video.video:description: The head of a Detroit automotive industry association said President Donald Trump’s latest round of tariffs on Canadian products could bring added uncertainty to carmakers.

video:video.video:title: Bystander hit by stray bullet during White House shooting sues Secret Service
video:video.video:description: An active-duty soldier on vacation in Washington who was hit by a stray bullet during a May shooting near the White House is suing the Secret Service over his life-threatening injuries.

video:video.video:title: 5 dead in attempt to save a struggling person in an Ohio river
video:video.video:description: An attempt to save a struggling swimmer in an Ohio river left five people dead over the weekend, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Independent autopsy of Nolan Wells finds ‘undetermined’ cause of death
video:video.video:description: An independent autopsy commissioned by the family of Nolan Xavier Wells found the 18-year-old’s cause and manner of death to be “undetermined” pending further investigation.

video:video.video:title: Mamdani says NYC can’t arrest Netanyahu, but the federal government should
video:video.video:description: Zohran Mamdani says New York City can't arrest Benjamin Netanyahu if he visits for the U.N. General Assembly, but he says the federal government should.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese Americans eager to visit ancestral homeland via direct flight for 1st time in decades
video:video.video:description: Some Lebanese Americans say they’re looking forward to avoiding the lengthy stopovers that were a part of their visits home before the White House reinstituted direct flights to Beirut (AP video by Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Mamdani restated NYC can't arrest Netanyahu for war crimes, urging the federal government to act
video:video.video:description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani reiterated that the city would not be able to execute an arrest warrant issued by the world’s top war-crimes court for Israel’s Benjamin Netanyahu, but urged the federal government to do so.

video:video.video:title: Protesters rally outside courthouse as Nicolás Maduro proceedings get underway
video:video.video:description: Nicolás Maduro has returned to a New York courthouse as his lawyers continue their objections to the U.S. military action that snatched Venezuela’s president from power.

video:video.video:title: National Guard is deployed as flash floods in West Virginia inundate homes and businesses
video:video.video:description: Parts of West Virginia are cut off after torrential rains inundated the state. National Guard members have been deployed to the hardest-hit areas in the northern part of the Mountain State.

video:video.video:title: Argentina fans welcome national team after World Cup loss vs Spain
video:video.video:description: Families and groups of friends battled the cold and the rain to show appreciation to a team that has been at the top of the international competitions for the last five years.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese troops begin deploying in southern village partially occupied by Israeli troops
video:video.video:description: The U.S. State Department announced Monday that operations in three villages deemed part of a "pilot zone" had begun, without giving details on what that entailed.

video:video.video:title: Rescue crews in Afghanistan search for missing, dead in rubble after flash floods
video:video.video:description: The Afghanistan National Disaster Management Authority said Tuesday that 23 people had died, 80 injured and more than 100 were missing after floods struck the eastern province of Nuristan on Monday, causing an undetermined number of buildings to collapse.

video:video.video:title: Yemen's Iranian-backed Houthi rebels say they will block Saudi shipping in Red Sea
video:video.video:description: Yemen’s Iranian-backed Houthi rebels on Monday announced they are imposing a maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia in retaliation for a blockade on Yemen and a recent attack on Sanaa International Airport, threatening another vital waterway for global shipping and raising fears of renewed conflict.

video:video.video:title: Italian border police celebrate seizure of massive haul of high-purity cocaine
video:video.video:description: Italy's border police on Tuesday seized almost 800 kilograms (1,763 lbs) of high-purity cocaine in the Vado Ligure port South of Genoa.

video:video.video:title: Eye and brainwave tech help artist with ALS find new stage as DJ
video:video.video:description: ALS, or amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, hasn’t stopped one artist in Tokyo from taking to the stage to perform with eye movements, AI and brain signals.

video:video.video:title: Cat nabbed: Russia prison officers catch feline being used as 'drug mule'
video:video.video:description: According to the Federal Penitentiary Service, the animal was carrying two homemade fabric collars containing prohibited substances that was intended to deliver them to inmates at Penal Colony No. 17.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 21
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, July 21st: Russia confirms military live-fire exercise in water off the UK; Rescue teams search for survivors of flash floods in Afghanistan; Cocaine shipment seized at port south of Genoa; World's-tallest nutcracker debuts in Minnesota.

video:video.video:title: Overnight Russian drone attack on Sumy leaves number of people injured
video:video.video:description: Overnight Russian drone attack on Sumy leaves number of people injured

video:video.video:title: OpenAI says its AI technology acted on its own in an ‘unprecedented’ hack of another company
video:video.video:description: OpenAI said Tuesday that its AI systems recently went rouge, breaking out of a testing environment and autonomously hacking into another AI company, a start up called Hugging Face.

video:video.video:title: June 1 trial date set for Venezuela's Nicolás Maduro in his US drug trafficking case
video:video.video:description: Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, have been held at a Brooklyn jail since U.S. forces seized them from their Caracas home and brought them to New York in early January.

video:video.video:title: Trump threatens new tariffs on Canada over wildfire smoke
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he is considering more tariffs on Canada over the wildfire smoke drifting into the United States, accusing Canada of mismanaging its forests.

video:video.video:title: Jake Johnson on filming in LA: 'You get really funny people'
video:video.video:description: Jake Johnson says his latest film, '"The Dink" has top comedy talent because they shot in L.A. The pickleball movie-- which Johnson also produced-- will be released on Apple TV July 24.

video:video.video:title: US sends food for Cuban residents while still pressuring island's government
video:video.video:description: A cargo plane packed with food and hygiene kits has left Miami for Cuba as part of the U.S. government’s humanitarian assistance efforts for the people of the island nation.

video:video.video:title: French lawmakers adopt a sweeping social media ban for children under 15
video:video.video:description: France is now the first country in the European Union to approve a blanket ban on the platforms as concerns grow worldwide over the harmful effects of digital content on kids.

video:video.video:title: Jennifer Garner's 'Pretend Cooking Show' to stay pretend
video:video.video:description: Jennifer Garner says a regular cooking show would start to feel manufactured. (July 21)

video:video.video:title: WTA introduces gene testing for all players on women’s tennis tour
video:video.video:description: The WTA will require one-time mandatory gene testing for all players on the women's tour under a new eligibility policy ahead of the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.

video:video.video:title: Luke Bracey 'blissfully ignorant' of Michael Landon's 'Little House' legacy
video:video.video:description: Luke Bracey went into playing Charles Ingalls with no prior knowledge of "Little House on the Prairie." (July 21)

video:video.video:title: A new soccer pitch for kids unveiled in metro Atlanta in an attempt to surf on World Cup wave
video:video.video:description: While no more World Cup games will be played in Atlanta, it didn’t mark the end of soccer for the host city.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 21
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, July 21st: Trump promises help for Lebanon during meeting with Aoun; Carney spoke to Trump over tariff threat; U.K. leader Burnham gathers Cabinet, removes Starmer supporters; Russian prison officers catch feline 'drug mule'.

video:video.video:title: Texas officer rescues man after car catches fire on the highway
video:video.video:description: A Southlake police officer rescued a man unharmed as his car caught fire on the highway in Southlake, Texas.

video:video.video:title: Latest on US-Iran war
video:video.video:description: A 10th straight day of attacks between the United States and Iran across the Middle East on Tuesday overshadowed diplomatic efforts in Pakistan to salvage an interim ceasefire deal that collapsed earlier this month as the two countries battled for control of a waterway vital to world energy supplies

video:video.video:title: New UK Prime Minister Burnham chairs first meeting with cabinet ministers
video:video.video:description: New UK Prime Minister Burnham chairs first meeting with cabinet ministers

video:video.video:title: Gas explosion kills at least 10 workers at hydropower project in India
video:video.video:description: At least 10 people were killed and 17 others remain missing after a suspected gas explosion triggered a collapse inside an under-construction tunnel at a hydropower project in India’s northeastern Himalayan state of Sikkim, officials said on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Conflicts and energy shocks threaten progress against global hunger, head of UN agency warns
video:video.video:description: The U.N. food security report, released Tuesday, found that global hunger has eased modestly in recent years, but projected that more than 500 million people could still face chronic hunger by 2030.

video:video.video:title: Momeni meets Pakistan officials as Iran and US continue attacks
video:video.video:description: Pakistan’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif met Tuesday with Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni and reiterated Islamabad’s offer to serve as an "honest and sincere mediator" to help ease tensions in the Gulf as diplomatic efforts intensified to halt the renewed Iran-U.S. conflict.

video:video.video:title: Anita-Joy Uwajeh on what 50 Cent brought to 'Fightland'
video:video.video:description: Curtis "50 Cent" Jackson is an executive producer on the new Starz drama "Fightland." The series premieres July 31 with new episodes streaming every Friday. (July 21)

video:video.video:title: US military completes 11th night of strikes on Iran as attacks overshadow diplomacy
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it completed the 11th night of strikes on Iran early Wednesday as attacks across the Middle East overshadowed diplomatic efforts in Pakistan to salvage an interim ceasefire deal that has collapsed.

video:video.video:title: See how the skeleton of a huge 240 million-year-old amphibian is cleaned
video:video.video:description: Unearthed in southern Poland in 2023, the fossil is the first Mastodonsaurus skeleton to be found in such a well-preserved, nearly complete state, according to Warsaw's Copernicus Science Center, which is hosting the citizen science project.

video:video.video:title: Israel builds giant earthen barrier inside the Gaza Strip
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military is quietly but quickly building a giant earthen barrier separating the over 50% of the Gaza Strip it controls from the rest of the territory, according to satellite imagery, further entrenching the division of the tiny Palestinian enclave.

video:video.video:title: Man tossed by bison recounts his experience: 'They move faster than you could ever imagine'
video:video.video:description: Carl McDaniel suffered multiple bone fractures after being thrown into the air by a bison in Yellowstone National Park on July 10.

video:video.video:title: Australian mother of four gives birth to identical quadruplet girls
video:video.video:description: An Australian woman has given birth to identical quadruplet girls, which gynecologists described as incredibly rare.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 22
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday July 22: Eleventh night of U.S. strikes in Iran; Remains of four service members to be returned to U.S.; Arizona Republicans nominate Trump ally for Governor; Tropical Storm Bertha approaches northern Gulf Coast.

video:video.video:title: ASEAN ministers joined by high-profile visitors in Manila
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Wednesday that Iran's demand to control and collect tolls in the Strait of Hormuz would threaten the world’s economy and the principle of freedom of navigation, even in Asia.

video:video.video:title: Private mission launches to extend life of out-of-gas communication satellites
video:video.video:description: A two-armed space robot blasted off on a private salvage operation Tuesday afternoon to slap life-extending jetpacks on old satellites running low on fuel thousands of miles up.

video:video.video:title: Death toll expected to rise as Guyana finds capsized ferry carried dozens more not on manifest list
video:video.video:description: One of the survivors of a ferry that capsized in Guyana over the weekend with as many as 179 people aboard recounted the harrowing moment when she held on to her 10-month-old son while in the water for 8 hours overnight.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian family split by Gaza war reunites after unimaginable loss
video:video.video:description: The last time Mais al-Gharam had seen her seven siblings was in the Gaza Strip a few months before the war.

video:video.video:title: Rubio explains latest US position on Iran to ASEAN counterparts
video:video.video:description: "As of now, despite their direct outreaches - and Iran has been reaching out to the United States, both directly and indirectly, to engage in talks about settling the differences - with regards to what's happening in that part of the world, unfortunately, so far, even though we have reached agreemen

video:video.video:title: Rubio meets Wang on sidelines of gathering of ASEAN ministers in Manila
video:video.video:description: It was the highest-level contact between the US and China since President Donald Trump flew to Beijing in May for talks with President Xi Jinping that focused on the war on Iran, trade and the status of Taiwan, among other issues.

video:video.video:title: Tributes paid to Kaylee Hottle, ''Godzilla' actor
video:video.video:description: Millie Bobby Brown, Rebecca Hall and Marlee Matlin are among the stars paying tribute to Kaylee Hottle, a deaf actor known for her work in two “Godzilla” films, who died in a car crash in Maryland on Tuesday, aged 18.

video:video.video:title: Comic-Con goes to the dogs at pet cosplay competition
video:video.video:description: Comic-Con week started with a woof, as over 500 people and their pets attended a cosplay extravaganza, Helen Woodward Animal Center's PAWmicon 2026 in San Diego.

video:video.video:title: NYC Mayor Mamdani says New York can't arrest Netanyahu but the federal government should
video:video.video:description: Mamdani had told the New York Times earlier this month that he and the city’s legal department were discussing arresting Netanyahu if he comes to New York City for the U.N.

video:video.video:title: Man tossed by Yellowstone bison suffers broken bones
video:video.video:description: A man who was thrown into the air by a bison in Yellowstone National Park says the animal didn't seem aggressive until it picked him as a target.

video:video.video:title: Tributes paid to Kaylee Hottle
video:video.video:description: Millie Bobby Brown, Rebecca Hall and Marlee Matlin are among the stars paying tribute to Kaylee Hottle, a deaf actor known for her work in two “Godzilla” films, who died in a car crash in Maryland on Tuesday, aged 18.

video:video.video:title: Motorcade believed to be carrying Maduro arrives at NY federal courthouse
video:video.video:description: Nicolás Maduro returned to court with his lawyers Wednesday and is expected to continue objecting to the U.S. military action that snatched the Venezuelan president from power and deposited him in New York to face drug trafficking charges.

video:video.video:title: Shania Twain embraces her "rasp" on new album
video:video.video:description: Shania Twain says vocal implant surgery gave her a new voice ahead of the release of new LP, "Little Miss Twain." (July 22)

video:video.video:title: Comic-Con goes to the dogs with pet cosplay
video:video.video:description: Comic-Con week started with a woof, as over 500 people and their pets attended a cosplay extravaganza, Helen Woodward Animal Center's PAWmicon 2026 in San Diego.

video:video.video:title: Rebel Wilson wins defamation case
video:video.video:description: Rebel Wilson has won a defamation lawsuit brought against her by Australian actor Charlotte MacInnes after a long dispute over a series of social media posts.

video:video.video:title: Lebanon PM demands Israel withdrawal during visit to south
video:video.video:description: Lebanon's prime minister Wednesday visited a battered southern village as a Lebanese-Israeli agreement brokered by Washington appears to have commenced.

video:video.video:title: Shania Twain looks back with new record, 'Little Miss Twain'
video:video.video:description: Shania Twain reflects on her humble beginnings and working with her son on her new album, "Little Miss Twain." (July 22)

video:video.video:title: Trump vows US will strike Iran's Pickaxe Mountain ‘very soon’
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. will soon strike the area near Pickaxe Mountain, an underground site near one of Iran's main nuclear facilities.

video:video.video:title: LIM KIM finds her own path
video:video.video:description: South Korean singer LIM KIM has released her second full-length album, "Exit to Nowhere," 13 years after her debut, "A Voice."

video:video.video:title: Supporters send food to India's 'Cockroach' protesters
video:video.video:description: People from all over India have been ordering food through delivery apps for protesters of the youth-led 'Cockroach' movement in New Delhi who have called for the resignation of India's Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over examination paper leaks.

video:video.video:title: AP reports from the Cockroach protests in New Delhi
video:video.video:description: What began as an online joke has evolved into a huge protest movement in India, led by young people hoping to change the established political structure and the damage they believe the government has caused.

video:video.video:title: Cocktails with a kick: Bagheera
video:video.video:description: How to make the Bagheera cocktail, served at Brutes of Mayfair, London. Inspired by the spicy marg, Brutes co-founder James Stevenson talks us through the recipe.

video:video.video:title: Louvre reopens Apollo Gallery after brazen heist, but no jewels on display
video:video.video:description: The Louvre's Apollo gallery on Wednesday reopened to the public for the first time since 88 million euros ($102 million) worth of crown jewels were stolen in a dramatic daytime heist.

video:video.video:title: Cocktails with a kick: Late Night
video:video.video:description: How to make the Late Night cocktail, served at FlipDog, London. Inspired by late night visits to the kebab shop, bar manager Alex Lyoness talks us through the recipe.

video:video.video:title: 'Spider-Man: Brand New Day' stars say they're a 'package deal' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Zendaya is shutting down the idea of continuing in "Spider-Man" films were Tom Holland to ever leave the franchise: "It's a package deal."

video:video.video:title: Divers clean Christ of the Abyss statue on seabed off coast of Italy
video:video.video:description: Italy's iconic underwater Christ of the Abyss statue has had an annual underwater clean.

video:video.video:title: Trump approves nuclear agreement that may allow Saudi Arabia to enrich uranium, two AP sources say
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump has approved an agreement with Saudi Arabia that could potentially provide the kingdom with uranium enrichment capability for its civilian nuclear program, according to two people familiar with the matter.
video:video.video:tag: Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud

video:video.video:title: Birthplace of Hitler in Austria reopens as police station to deter Nazi pilgrimages
video:video.video:description: Adolf Hitler's birthplace in Austria was inaugurated as a police station on Wednesday in an effort to destroy the building's appeal as a site of pilgrimage for people who glorify the Nazi dictator.

video:video.video:title: June 1 trial date set for Venezuela's Nicolás Maduro in his US drug trafficking case
video:video.video:description: Maduro, 63, and his wife, Cilia Flores, 69, have been held at a Brooklyn jail since U.S. forces seized them from their Caracas home in a middle-of-the-night raid and brought them to New York in early January.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine military leadership overhauled following protests in Kyiv
video:video.video:description: The top-level changes late Tuesday were intended to draw a line under political turmoil that lasted almost a week.

video:video.video:title: Spain PM Sanchez visits wildfire-hit areas in northeast, meets emergency workers
video:video.video:description: Firefighters in central Spain on Wednesday battled a wildfire that has burned over 32,000 hectares (79,000 acres) in one of the largest blazes in recent memory for the southern European country.

video:video.video:title: Secret Service says they are prepared for Trump to attend rescheduled press dinner
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secret Service says they are prepared for President Donald Trump to attend the rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Association dinner this week.

video:video.video:title: OpenAI AI models hacked another company on their own
video:video.video:description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI said Tuesday that its artificial intelligence system hacked into another AI company on its own in what the company called an “unprecedented cyber incident.”

video:video.video:title: Trump pays respects to 4 US service members killed in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump honored four U.S. service members killed in the Middle East during a dignified transfer at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware on Wednesday, the third such ceremony he has attended since launching the war in Iran in February.

video:video.video:title: Trial date set for Venezuela's Nicolás Maduro in his US drug trafficking case
video:video.video:description: Maduro, 63, and his wife, Cilia Flores, 69, have been held at a Brooklyn jail since U.S. forces seized them from their Caracas home in a middle-of-the-night raid and brought them to New York in early January.

video:video.video:title: Independent autopsy of Nolan Wells finds 'undetermined' cause of death
video:video.video:description: An independent autopsy commissioned by the family of Nolan Xavier Wells found the 18-year-old’s cause and manner of death to be “undetermined” pending further investigation.

video:video.video:title: What to know about Iran's Pickaxe Mountain, home to an underground nuclear site threatened by Trump
video:video.video:description: The site, known as Kuh-e Kolang Gaz La, or “Pickaxe Mountain,” lies on the south side of Iran's Natanz nuclear enrichment site, which was bombed by the United States during Operation Midnight Hammer in 2025's 12-day war between Iran and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Trump warns Iran will 'pay a big price' as he heads to Dover to honor fallen service members
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Iran "is gonna pay a big price" before departing for Dover Air Force Base to attend the dignified transfer of four U.S. service members killed in the Middle East, calling it "one of the hardest things to do as a president."

video:video.video:title: 'The last picture': A passing boater's photograph may be the 5 adults who drowned in Ohio
video:video.video:description: Pastor Marcus L. Martin felt instant trepidation when he saw five adults wading hip- and waist-deep in the Scioto River last weekend.

video:video.video:title: Surveillance video shows fireworks and flames lit on New York federal building
video:video.video:description: Surveillance footage released Wednesday by the U.S. Attorney's Office shows a former U.S. Army soldier shooting off fireworks, starting a fire and shooting a pellet gun outside a Manhattan federal office building before he was subdued Monday morning.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 22
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, July 22nd: Trump pays respects to U.S. service members killed; Venezuela's Maduro gets June 1 trial date in drug trafficking case; Firefighters battle huge Spanish wildfire amid heat wave; Louvre reopens Apollo Gallery after brazen heist.

video:video.video:title: Wisconsin police officer shoots, kills man on city street near state Capitol in Madison
video:video.video:description: A Madison police officer shot and killed a man who had injured him with a knife while resisting arrest, the city's police chief said Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Independent autopsy of Nolan Wells finds 'undetermined' cause of death
video:video.video:description: The preliminary findings noted that the decomposition of the body, along with other limiting factors, had made it impossible to rule out if foul play contributed to or caused the death of Wells.

video:video.video:title: D4vd preliminary hearing hears testimony from former money manager
video:video.video:description: The preliminary hearing for singer D4vd continued Wednesday in downtown Los Angeles. The attorney for Celeste Rivas Hernandez's family called it a "much better day" for her parents after testimony from D4vd's former money manager focused on the singer's earnings.

video:video.video:title: Extensive wildfires hit several towns in Sicily, Italy
video:video.video:description: Firefighters and residents worked to put out wildfires across various towns on the Italian island of Sicily.

video:video.video:title: Residents in Sicily areas urged to evacuate homes as wildfires rage
video:video.video:description: Several wildfires burned across the Italian island of Sicily on Wednesday, prompting the evacuation of residents as firefighters worked to contain the spread.

video:video.video:title: Louvre reopens Apollo Gallery after brazen heist, but no jewels on display
video:video.video:description: The Louvre's Apollo gallery reopened to the public for the first time since last year's crown jewels heist, but the precious jewels it once displayed have been removed.

video:video.video:title: Rebel Wilson wins defamation case brought by former co-star
video:video.video:description: Rebel Wilson has won a defamation lawsuit brought against her by Australian actor Charlotte MacInnes after a long dispute over a series of social media posts. (July 22)

video:video.video:title: West Virginia governor says some are unaccounted for in flooding
video:video.video:description: West Virginia Governor Patrick Morrisey gives an update on the state's massive flooding during a press conference in Buckhannon, West Virginia.

video:video.video:title: New Hawaii law tries to balance truck bed-riding safety with island charms
video:video.video:description: Riding on the back of trucks has long been part of island life in Hawaii, but as roads have become faster and more dangerous, worries about the safety of riding truck beds, which have no seatbelts, have increased.

video:video.video:title: Former aid workers turn to sex work after US cuts foreign aid
video:video.video:description: Former HIV prevention workers in Nepal say the loss of funding through USAID left them with few options for survival, with some, including transgender employees who once helped combat HIV, turning to sex work.

video:video.video:title: 'Nice hairstyle': Russian foreign minister quips at reporter after Rubio meeting
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio met with Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov in the Philippines.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the 'Cockroach party' movement in India
video:video.video:description: Calling themselves the “Cockroaches,” young Indians are channeling anger over failings in the education system into a broader campaign for government accountability.

video:video.video:title: Adolf Hitler’s birthplace in Austria reopens as a police station
video:video.video:description: Austria home where Hitler was born reopens as police station

video:video.video:title: US military carries out strikes on Iran as both sides threaten civilian infrastructure
video:video.video:description: U.S. Central Command said the attacks were designed to “further degrade Iran’s ability to threaten civilian mariners and commercial vessels transiting regional waters,” as the Americans push to regain control over the Strait of Hormuz and restore the flow of international shipping.
